hypothesis: A hybrid batching strategy that uses the dynamic algorithm for the initial 100,000 training steps and then switches to static-64 for the remainder will achieve a lower total wall-clock time to reach within 1% of the converged test RMSE than using either the dynamic or static-64 algorithm exclusively on datasets with node-count excess kurtosis > 1 (e.g., AFLOW) when training PaiNN or MPEU models on a single NVIDIA A100 GPU with the Adam optimizer.
